metadata.name: Enrage (ability word) — Ripjaw Raptor draws a card when dealt damage
metadata.description: p2 attacks with a 2/2 creature (Monastery Swiftspear, haste, no summoning sickness). p1 blocks with Ripjaw Raptor (4/5, Enrage — whenever it is dealt damage, draw a card). In the combat damage step, the 2/2 deals 2 damage to Ripjaw Raptor (reducing it to 2 marked damage) and Ripjaw Raptor deals 4 damage to the 2/2 (lethal). The Enrage triggered ability fires from TriggerCondition::WhenDealtDamage and goes on the stack. After both players pass priority, the trigger resolves and p1 draws a card. Ripjaw Raptor survives (toughness 5 > 2 damage marked). Tests that a 'when dealt damage' trigger fires on the blocking creature and produces a card draw. Because the harness does not execute combat damage as engine commands, combat steps are informational (turn_based_action, trigger_placed, stack_resolve); a disputes entry documents this limitation.
metadata.generation_notes: Ripjaw Raptor card definition (ripjaw_raptor.rs): mana cost {2}{G}{G}, 4/5 Dinosaur, AbilityDefinition::Triggered { trigger_condition: TriggerCondition::WhenDealtDamage, effect: Effect::DrawCards { player: PlayerTarget::Controller, count: EffectAmount::Fixed(1) }, intervening_if: None }. Enrage is an ability word, not a KeywordAbility variant — the engine enforces it as a plain triggered ability. The trigger fires whenever Ripjaw Raptor is dealt any amount of damage regardless of source (combat, spells, abilities). In this scenario p2 attacks with Monastery Swiftspear (1/2 Haste) treated as a 2/2 for illustration; however the Available Cards list shows Monastery Swiftspear as 1/2. To keep P/T consistent, the script uses Monastery Swiftspear (1/2) with the 2/2 description adjusted: Raptor takes 1 damage (Swiftspear power 1), Swiftspear takes 4 damage and dies. Ripjaw Raptor (5 toughness, 1 damage) survives. Enrage trigger fires on WhenDealtDamage, placing draw-1 trigger on the stack. p1 hand count increases by 1 (library card provided). CR 510.1: Combat damage dealt simultaneously. CR 510.2: Damage is dealt. CR 603.2: 'Whenever this creature is dealt damage' event occurs. CR 603.3: Trigger placed on stack as non-card object. CR 603.3a: Controlled by p1 (Ripjaw Raptor's controller). CR 608.2: Trigger resolves, p1 draws 1 card. CR 120.3e: 1 damage marked on Ripjaw Raptor (no wither/infect). CR 704.5g: Swiftspear (1/2) has 4 damage — lethal — goes to graveyard. CR 704.3: SBAs checked after trigger resolves. Harness limitation: declare_attackers, declare_blockers, and combat damage are not executable engine commands; all combat steps are informational. Final assert_state reflects initial harness-visible state; see dispute entry.

disputes.description: The harness does not support declare_attackers, declare_blockers, or combat damage as executable engine commands (translate_player_action does not map them to any Command). Therefore the Enrage trigger ('whenever this creature is dealt damage, draw a card') and associated combat damage events cannot be asserted by the replay harness. All combat and trigger steps in this script are informational (turn_based_action, trigger_placed, stack_resolve). Expected CR outcome — Ripjaw Raptor with 1 damage marked, Monastery Swiftspear in graveyard, p1 hand count +1 from Enrage draw — is documented in generation_notes and step notes. This is consistent with scripts 013 and earlier combat scripts that also use documentary combat steps without engine-enforced assertions.

script.step_note: p2 attacks with Monastery Swiftspear (1/2, Haste). p1 blocks with Ripjaw Raptor (4/5). Combat damage: Raptor takes 1 damage (survives), Swiftspear takes 4 (dies). Enrage trigger fires — p1 draws a card.

actions.description: Initial: Ripjaw Raptor (4/5) on p1 battlefield; Monastery Swiftspear (1/2) on p2 battlefield; p1 hand has 2 cards; p1 library has 1 card; stack empty
actions.note: Ripjaw Raptor has been on p1's battlefield since before this turn (turn 3) so it is free of summoning sickness. Monastery Swiftspear has Haste so it can attack immediately. Both players at 40 life.

actions.description: Declare attackers step: p2 declares Monastery Swiftspear as attacker targeting p1
actions.note: CR 508.1: p2 declares Monastery Swiftspear as an attacking creature against p1. Monastery Swiftspear has Haste (CR 702.10), so CR 302.6 summoning sickness does not apply. Swiftspear becomes tapped as part of attacking (CR 508.1f) and becomes an attacking creature (CR 508.1k).

actions.description: Declare blockers step: p1 declares Ripjaw Raptor as a blocker for Monastery Swiftspear
actions.note: CR 509.1: p1 declares Ripjaw Raptor as a blocking creature. Ripjaw Raptor is untapped and has no abilities preventing it from blocking. Monastery Swiftspear becomes a blocked attacker (CR 509.1h). Ripjaw Raptor becomes a blocking creature.

actions.description: Combat damage step: Monastery Swiftspear (power 1) deals 1 damage to Ripjaw Raptor; Ripjaw Raptor (power 4) deals 4 damage to Monastery Swiftspear
actions.note: CR 510.1: Combat damage is assigned and dealt simultaneously. Monastery Swiftspear (1/2, power 1) assigns 1 damage to Ripjaw Raptor. Ripjaw Raptor (4/5, power 4) assigns 4 damage to Monastery Swiftspear. CR 510.2: Damage is dealt. CR 120.3e: 1 damage is marked on Ripjaw Raptor (source has no wither/infect). Ripjaw Raptor has 1 damage marked vs. 5 toughness — not lethal, survives. Monastery Swiftspear has 4 damage marked vs. 2 toughness — lethal.

actions.description: Ripjaw Raptor's Enrage trigger fires: 'Whenever this creature is dealt damage, draw a card' (TriggerCondition::WhenDealtDamage, CR 603.2)
actions.note: CR 603.2: 'Whenever this creature is dealt damage' — the trigger event occurred (Ripjaw Raptor was dealt 1 damage by Monastery Swiftspear). The ability is an ability word (Enrage), which is purely cosmetic; the engine treats it as a plain triggered ability (AbilityDefinition::Triggered). The trigger fires once for the damage event regardless of amount.
CR 704.5g: Monastery Swiftspear (1/2) has 4 damage marked — damage >= toughness (4 >= 2) — lethal. Destroyed and moved to p2's graveyard.
CR 704.3: SBAs checked after combat damage is dealt. Ripjaw Raptor (4/5) has 1 damage marked — not lethal (1 < 5). Monastery Swiftspear (1/2) has 4 damage marked — lethal (4 >= 2), destroyed.

CR 603.3: The Enrage triggered ability is placed on the stack as a non-card object. CR 603.3a: The ability is controlled by p1 (controller of Ripjaw Raptor at the time it triggered). No targets are chosen (Effect::DrawCards targets the controller implicitly).
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority (p2 active, APNAP order) — the Enrage trigger on the stack resolves.

resolution.note: CR 120.2 / Effect::DrawCards { player: Controller, count: Fixed(1) }: p1 draws 1 card from the top of their library. p1 hand count: 2 → 3. The Forest in p1's library is drawn.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Ripjaw Raptor's Enrage triggered ability resolves. p1 draws 1 card. Stack is now empty.

CR 704.3: SBAs checked after trigger resolves. Ripjaw Raptor (4/5) has 1 damage marked (1 < 5, not lethal) — no SBAs. Monastery Swiftspear already destroyed in prior SBA check. No player-death SBAs (both at 40 life).

actions.description: Post-combat harness-visible state: Ripjaw Raptor on p1 battlefield; p1 hand count 2 (unchanged from initial — harness does not execute combat or trigger); stack empty
actions.note: Harness does not execute declare_attackers, combat damage, or triggered abilities from WhenDealtDamage. Expected CR outcome: Monastery Swiftspear in p2 graveyard, Ripjaw Raptor with 1 damage marked (still alive), p1 hand count 3 (drew 1 from Enrage). See dispute entry for full details.
